---
title: "Synthetic Data Reveals Generalization Gaps in Correlated Multiple Instance Learning"
description: "A Bayes-optimal synthetic benchmark exposes a generalization gap between correlated and non-correlated MIL architectures."
author:
  - name: Ethan Harvey
  - name: Dennis Johan Loevlie
  - name: Michael C. Hughes
date: 2025-12-09
date-modified: last-modified
categories: [multiple-instance-learning, generalization, ml4h]
---

::: {.paper-links}
[Paper](https://arxiv.org/abs/2510.25759){.btn}
[arXiv](https://arxiv.org/abs/2510.25759){.btn}
[BibTeX](#bibtex){.btn}
:::

::: {.tldr}
**TL;DR.** Multiple-instance learning architectures that explicitly model
inter-instance correlations *underperform* simpler non-correlated baselines on
held-out distributions when correlations were learned rather than imposed by
the architecture. We expose the gap with a synthetic benchmark whose
Bayes-optimal classifier we can compute exactly.
:::

## Abstract

Multiple instance learning (MIL) is the standard formulation when bag-level
labels gate instance-level features — pathology slides, ECG records, drug
interaction graphs. Recent attention- and transformer-based MIL methods
explicitly model inter-instance correlations. We construct a synthetic
benchmark in which the Bayes-optimal classifier is analytically known and
show that correlated MIL architectures *generalize worse* than non-correlated
baselines despite higher in-distribution accuracy.

## Why it matters

The result inverts a common assumption — that explicit correlation modeling
is always at least neutral. We localize the failure to a specific class of
distribution shifts, give a closed-form description of the gap on the
synthetic family, and show that the same pattern reproduces on three
real-world MIL benchmarks (Camelyon16, MUSK1, MUSK2).

## Cite this work {#bibtex}

::: {.bibtex}
<details>
<summary>Copy BibTeX</summary>
```bibtex
@inproceedings{harvey2025synthetic,
  author    = {Harvey, Ethan and Loevlie, Dennis Johan and Hughes, Michael C.},
  title     = {Synthetic Data Reveals Generalization Gaps in Correlated Multiple Instance Learning},
  booktitle = {ML4H 2025 Symposium (Findings Track)},
  year      = {2025},
  url       = {https://arxiv.org/abs/2510.25759}
}
```
</details>
:::
